source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The portion of the string of a certain musical instrument between the bridge and upper end of the finger board has mass \( 2.00\, \text{g} \). The string sounds an \( A_4 \) note (\( 440\, \text{Hz} \)) when played. Where must the player put a finger (what distance \( x \) from the bridge) to play a \( D_5 \) note (\( 587\, \text{Hz} \))? For both the \( A_4 \) and \( D_5 \) notes, the string vibrates in its fundamental mode.

image_dependency: asset registry shows 1 associated image and registry_passed=true
question text itself contains the key quantities and target variable, with no explicit reference to reading values from the image, so image dependence is not clearly high
multi_step: solving requires combining the fundamental-mode relation with the frequency change from A4 (440 Hz) to D5 (587 Hz)
the player position x must be inferred from the new vibrating length relative to the original string segment
multiple-choice options are provided, but the task still involves at least a two-step physics-to-numeric derivation
verifiability: answer format is a short numeric text with unit-like content ('45cm')
choices are provided, enabling straightforward option-level checking
the problem is a standard quantitative physics item with a determinate result under the stated assumptions, and asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true

decision_reasons: Core assets are complete: question, answer, and associated image are all present, and registry_passed=true with no recorded asset issues.
